# VantaDB — AI Knowledge File
> Visit https://vantadb.dev for the full site.

## What is VantaDB?
VantaDB is an open-source embedded vector database that unifies SQL, vector search (HNSW + IVF), and full-text search (BM25) in a single Rust binary. MIT licensed.

## Quick facts
- **Embedded**: No server process. Runs in-process like SQLite.
- **Performance**: p50 latency 1.2ms, Recall@10 0.998, 1172 bytes/vector
- **License**: MIT open core
- **SDKs**: Python (pip install vantadb-py), Rust (cargo add vantadb), C API
- **Platforms**: Linux, macOS, Windows, Android (NDK), iOS (XCFramework)

## Key pages
- [Documentation](/docs) — Quickstart, installation, SDK guides
- [Engine](/engine) — HNSW, IVF, BM25, hybrid RRF
- [Architecture](/architecture) — WAL, storage engine, query pipeline
- [Pricing](/pricing) — Free (MIT), Pro ($49/mo), Enterprise (custom)
- [Changelog](/changelog) — Release history v0.4.0 → v0.6.0
- [Roadmap](/about/roadmap) — Engineering milestones and timeline

## Solutions
- [AI Agents](/solutions/ai-agents) — Persistent memory for LLM agents
- [Local RAG](/solutions/local-rag) — On-device RAG, zero data leaks
- [AI IDE Tooling](/solutions/ai-ide-tooling) — Codebase intelligence

## Key differentiators
1. **No cloud dependency** — YOUR data never leaves YOUR network
2. **Three query engines** — SQL + vector + full-text, hybrid RRF fusion
3. **Zero ops** — No servers, no clusters, no per-vector pricing
4. **Sub-millisecond** — 1.2ms p50 hybrid search latency
